text.title: EU AI Act Compliance Checklist by Phase
text.summary: Phased compliance checklist: GPAI obligations (Aug 2025), high-risk system requirements (Aug 2026), and deployer duties.

rule.statement: Prepare technical documentation, model cards, and training data summaries before August 2025 for all GPAI models.
rule.rationale: GPAI obligations are the first hard deadline after the prohibited-practices ban.

list.item: Prepare technical documentation for GPAI models including capabilities and limitations
Create model cards with architecture, training methodology, and evaluation results
Document training data sources, types, and preprocessing
Implement copyright compliance (respect robots.txt, opt-out mechanisms)
For systemic risk (>10^25 FLOPs): red-team, assess systemic risks, track incidents

rule.statement: High-risk AI providers must implement all seven Article requirements and register in the EU AI database before deployment.
rule.rationale: Article 9–17 requirements are cumulative; incomplete implementation invalidates conformity assessment.

list.item: Article 9: Risk management system — identify, estimate, mitigate, test residual risks
Article 10: Data governance — document training data, verify quality, address bias
Article 11: Technical documentation — system description, design specs, test results
Article 12: Record-keeping — automatic logging, traceable events, defined retention
Article 13: Transparency — instructions for use, capabilities, limitations disclosed
Article 14: Human oversight — override capability designed in, not bolted on
Article 15: Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity — adversarial attack mitigations
Article 17: Quality management system — compliance strategy, corrective actions
Article 43: Conformity assessment — self-assessment or third-party per category
Article 71: EU database registration before deployment
Verify provider compliance documentation before integration
Implement human oversight measures as specified in provider instructions
Monitor AI system operation and report malfunctions to provider
Conduct fundamental rights impact assessment (where required by sector)
Inform workers and affected individuals of AI use

rule.statement: Chatbots must disclose AI nature at conversation start; AI-generated images/video must carry machine-readable watermarks.
rule.rationale: Users have the right to know when interacting with AI; content authenticity enables fact-checking.

list.item: Chatbots: Disclose AI nature clearly at first interaction; offer human escalation
Synthetic content: Add C2PA-compatible machine-readable watermarks
Emotion recognition: Inform subjects; obtain consent; comply with GDPR
Track AI system performance and monitor for drift or degradation
Maintain and update technical documentation with every model change
Establish incident reporting process with defined severity thresholds
Report serious incidents to national authorities within required timelines
